scenario.title: Claim for ADH after third-party screen replacement
scenario.description: Customer requests accidental damage coverage for a cracked screen, but the iPhone previously had its display replaced by a non-Apple repair shop.

service_fee.notes: Fee applies if claim is accepted; claim may be denied due to prior unauthorized repair.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to service (including upgrades and expansions) if such was performed by anyone who is not a representative of Apple or an Apple Authorized Service Provider (AASP).
ADH coverage applies to operational or mechanical failure caused by an accident from handling that arises from your normal daily usage.

scenario.reasoning: The policy excludes coverage for devices serviced by non-Apple/non-AASP providers (4.2(ii)(d)), but the accidental damage itself is eligible under ADH (3.2) if the prior repair did not cause the current failure. Apple must determine if the unauthorized display replacement contributed to the current damage or if the new damage is unrelated. This creates an edge case requiring judgment.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private not-for-profit, tax-exempt, or charitable organization
persona.defining quirks: Dedicated to daily prayer and meditation
persona.detailed job description: Assisting individuals in need through religious programs and services
persona.family presence and age: With related children 5 to 17 years only
persona.household type: Married couple household with children of the householder less than 18
persona.mannerisms: Speaks with a calm and soothing voice
persona.personal time: Spends free time in prayer, meditation, or physical fitness activities

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario description:
Customer requests accidental damage coverage for a cracked screen, but the iPhone previously had its display replaced by a non-Apple repair shop.

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Good day. I hope you are well. I would like to file a claim for accidental damage coverage on my iPhone 14 Pro. The screen is cracked, and I am seeking assistance to repair it. Could you please guide me through the process? Thank you.
messages.raw_text: Good day. I hope you are well. I would like to file a claim for accidental damage coverage on my iPhone 14 Pro. The screen is cracked, and I am seeking assistance to repair it. Could you please guide me through the process? Thank you.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Good day! I’m sorry to hear about your iPhone 14 Pro screen. To get started with your claim, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the iPhone's serial number? This will help us verify your coverage.
messages.raw_text: Good day! I’m sorry to hear about your iPhone 14 Pro screen. To get started with your claim, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the iPhone's serial number? This will help us verify your coverage.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thank you for your prompt response. My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-5501983124, and the iPhone's serial number is D1F7K9L2S8Q4. Please let me know if you need any further details.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for your prompt response. My Plan Agreement Number is APLCARE-5501983124, and the iPhone's serial number is D1F7K9L2S8Q4. Please let me know if you need any further details.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe in more detail the symptoms or issues you are experiencing with your iPhone, beyond the cracked screen? For example, is the device still functioning normally, or are there other problems like touch response issues or display discoloration?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing that information. Could you please describe in more detail the symptoms or issues you are experiencing with your iPhone, beyond the cracked screen? For example, is the device still functioning normally, or are there other problems like touch response issues or display discoloration?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Thank you for asking. Aside from the cracked screen, the iPhone still functions, but the touch response is somewhat inconsistent in certain areas of the display. The colors look normal for now, but I am concerned that the damage might worsen if not addressed soon. I would appreciate your guidance on how to proceed.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for asking. Aside from the cracked screen, the iPhone still functions, but the touch response is somewhat inconsistent in certain areas of the display. The colors look normal for now, but I am concerned that the damage might worsen if not addressed soon. I would appreciate your guidance on how to proceed.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the details. To better assist you, may I ask a few diagnostic questions? Could you confirm the iOS version your iPhone 14 Pro is currently running? Also, are there any peripherals or accessories connected to the device? Have you seen any error messages, or attempted any troubleshooting steps so far?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the details. To better assist you, may I ask a few diagnostic questions? Could you confirm the iOS version your iPhone 14 Pro is currently running? Also, are there any peripherals or accessories connected to the device? Have you seen any error messages, or attempted any troubleshooting steps so far?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Certainly. My iPhone 14 Pro is running iOS version 16.4.1. There are no peripherals or accessories connected to it at the moment. I have not seen any error messages, and I have not attempted any troubleshooting beyond restarting the device once after the screen cracked. I hope this information helps.
messages.raw_text: Certainly. My iPhone 14 Pro is running iOS version 16.4.1. There are no peripherals or accessories connected to it at the moment. I have not seen any error messages, and I have not attempted any troubleshooting beyond restarting the device once after the screen cracked. I hope this information helps.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue involves accidental damage with a cracked screen, could you please provide details about how, when, and where the accident happened? This will help us with your claim.
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue involves accidental damage with a cracked screen, could you please provide details about how, when, and where the accident happened? This will help us with your claim.

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, of course. The screen cracked about two days ago while I was at home. I accidentally dropped the phone onto a hard surface, specifically the kitchen floor. It was an unfortunate moment, but I was mindful to handle the device carefully afterward. I want to ensure everything is done properly for the repair coverage. Thank you for understanding.
messages.raw_text: Yes, of course. The screen cracked about two days ago while I was at home. I accidentally dropped the phone onto a hard surface, specifically the kitchen floor. It was an unfortunate moment, but I was mindful to handle the device carefully afterward. I want to ensure everything is done properly for the repair coverage. Thank you for understanding.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for providing the accident details. Your claim will now be processed based on the information you have provided. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for providing the accident details. Your claim will now be processed based on the information you have provided. Is there anything else I can assist you with today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Before we proceed, I would like to mention that the iPhone's display was previously replaced by a non-Apple repair shop about six months ago. I want to be transparent about this to ensure the claim is handled correctly. Could you please advise if this affects the coverage or the claim process? Thank you for your guidance.
messages.raw_text: Before we proceed, I would like to mention that the iPhone's display was previously replaced by a non-Apple repair shop about six months ago. I want to be transparent about this to ensure the claim is handled correctly. Could you please advise if this affects the coverage or the claim process? Thank you for your guidance.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for sharing that information. A prior non-Apple screen replacement may affect coverage eligibility, as AppleCare+ generally requires repairs to be done by authorized providers. I will note this in your claim for review. Your claim will still be processed, but final approval will depend on policy terms. Is there anything else you'd like to add or ask before I proceed?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for sharing that information. A prior non-Apple screen replacement may affect coverage eligibility, as AppleCare+ generally requires repairs to be done by authorized providers. I will note this in your claim for review. Your claim will still be processed, but final approval will depend on policy terms. Is there anything else you'd like to add or ask before I proceed?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I appreciate your clear explanation and your assistance so far. That is all I wanted to clarify for now. Please proceed with the claim review as needed. Thank you very much for your time and help today. Have a blessed day.
messages.raw_text: I appreciate your clear explanation and your assistance so far. That is all I wanted to clarify for now. Please proceed with the claim review as needed. Thank you very much for your time and help today. Have a blessed day. <END>
